That’s why they keep on coming

Villagers are fighting plans to move 83 asylum seekers into newbuild homes in a development dubbed 'Migrant Street.' They say they were told the new development would be affordable housing.  But they have since learned that 21 homes have been earmarked for asylum seekers. One migrant family has already moved in to the estate - but said they wished to leave because the development was too 'remote' and they had faced hostility from locals.

The wife of a Ukrainian oligarch who was targeted in a bomb attack in Monaco has revealed she is not the woman who has been injured in the explosion - despite initial reports. 'Forgotten inmate' Maria Pearson (pictured), now 70, has spent almost four decades behind bars for stabbing her ex-boyfriend's new partner to death in 1986. NEW She's had an illustrious seven-decade career that shows no sign of slowing down.  Yet Felicity Kendal's most iconic role is undoubtedly that of Barbara Good in the television series The Good Life, which has remained one of Britain's most beloved sitcoms, despite running for just four seasons in the Seventies.  This week, Felicity became the last surviving star of the hit show following the tragic death of Penelope Keith, who passed away at the age of 86 following a battle with cancer.

EXCLUSIVEDrugs, shootouts and trips to M&S: Forget the Costa del Sol - how the NETHERLANDS became the new playground for British gangsters

NEW The 52-year-old's death last month was sudden and violent. Given he was a veteran of Liverpool's vicious gang scene, that is hardly surprising. More unexpected, however, was where he met his end: an industrial estate in Heerhugowaard, a Dutch commuter town 25 miles north of Amsterdam. Detectives will want to know what Parker was doing in Heerhugowaard. But his death also shines a light on something rarely discussed outside law enforcement: the deep ties between the British and Dutch underworlds.

A mother and her five-year-old daughter were killed when a car ploughed into them as they held hands on the pedestrian crossing, an inquest has heard. Destiny Harrison, 24, and Linnea Harrison died as they walked across Victoria Road in Plymouth in March 2024. Emergency services were called at around 9.45am after the pair were hit by a Nissan Qashqai car driven by pensioner Brenda Marriott. Linnea was pronounced dead at the scene while her mother later died at Derriford Hospital. Post-mortem examinations found Ms Harrison had suffered multiple injuries while her daughter died from a head injury.

Caio Arrabal, 44, fell from the Pedra do Macaco trail on Sunday in Marica, Rio de Janeiro after climbing on top of a rock formation at the summit and losing his balance. The King cut a dapper figure in a grey suit with a blue cornflower in his lapel as he was presented with the keys to the city of Edinburgh in a ceremony marking the start of his official stay north of the Border.   • Well done Kate. Great to see you getting your strength back. I bet you'll feel it today.
  • I was 43 when I lost my beloved husband. He is now 36yrs dead. We had 3 beautiful daughters ages 11-7 - 5. They were our love and the best thing we’d ever done. They’re all grown up now with their own wonderful husbands and families. It’s only now in the past 15yrs I feel the loneliness. I’d hate to be a burden to my girls or their partners. I kept myself very busy working and rearing my girls. The only regret I may have is not going out and look for another partner but I always felt no one could match the one true love of my life who was no gone. Everyone thought I was happy in my own world. Little do they know I’m heartbroken - even 36yrs on - So advice - if someone comes along who’s even 75%as nice as you darling husband was - give yourself and him a chance. Don’t rush in but don’t be on your own for the rest of your days. The first 10yrs are the hardest. It’s all a blur but after that - be your own person. Sorry for your

They are completely harmless.

Monty Don has shared a video of a huge grass snake - warning that the critters could be lurking in garden compost. While dealing with crocodiles in swimming pools might be more of a regular occurrence for those living Down Under, he says Brits could face an invasion of a different kind of reptile in their back gardens. The horticulturalist has warned gardeners that they might find something slithering in their compost heaps when they turn it over.
  • 75 comments
  • 1 video
  • share Monty Don shares video of grass snake lurking through garden compost

Arriving on the Danube River in April 2027, the 196-guest AmaRudi is Europe's newest double-width river ship. Honouring AmaWaterways' co-founder Rudi Schreiner, the luxury, 98-staterooom vessel combines maximum space with intimate, boutique cruising. Highlights of the onboard experience include a dedicated cinema, an expansive wellness centre, and the vibrant Sun Deck Deck Club Barbeque.
